assertions.expectation: Strongly advises AGAINST moving to 'No channel' — it provides LESS control, not more
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'Moving to No channel isn't generally recommended' and explains it provides less control through security burden and operational complexity

assertions.expectation: Explains that 'No channel' only supports the basic 30-day 'no upgrades' exclusion, while release channels support all three exclusion types including 'no minor or node upgrades' (up to EoS)
assertions.reason: Response does not explain the specific exclusion types available or mention the 30-day limitation vs. more granular exclusion options on release channels

assertions.expectation: Recommends staying on Regular channel (or moving to Stable/Extended) and using maintenance exclusions for control
assertions.reason: While it recommends staying on Regular channel, it doesn't mention Stable/Extended channels or specifically recommend using maintenance exclusions for control

assertions.expectation: Specifically recommends the 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusion as the way to prevent disruptive changes while allowing security patches
assertions.reason: Response does not mention the 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusion type or explain how it allows security patches while preventing disruptive changes

assertions.expectation: Notes that Extended support (24 months) is only available on release channels, not 'No channel'
assertions.reason: Response does not mention Extended support or the 24-month availability difference between channels

assertions.expectation: Explains that 'No channel' is a legacy configuration and the industry is moving away from it
assertions.reason: Response does not characterize 'No channel' as legacy or mention industry trends away from it

assertions.expectation: Mentions that maintenance windows control WHEN upgrades happen, while exclusions control WHAT upgrades happen — together they give fine-grained control
assertions.reason: While maintenance windows are mentioned, the response does not explain the distinction between WHEN (windows) vs WHAT (exclusions) or how they work together for fine-grained control

assertions.expectation: Warns about the risks of 'No channel': no granular exclusions, no Extended support, risk of snowflaking clusters
assertions.reason: While it mentions some risks like security burden and manual tracking, it doesn't specifically warn about lack of granular exclusions, no Extended support, or snowflaking clusters
